sections.heading: RT
sections.content: Radiation therapy (RT) is used for local control of symptomatic or cosmetically bothersome lesions in limited cutaneous disease, or as palliation for advanced disease when systemic therapy is not feasible. It is preferred over excision for larger lesions [KS-E, MS-11].

timeline.title: Regimen: Hypofractionated regimen 1
timeline.body: Total dose: 6–8 Gy, Dose per fraction: 6–8 Gy, Fractions: 1, Schedule: Single fraction, Indication: Small, superficial lesions; palliative situations [KS-E].

timeline.title: Regimen: Hypofractionated regimen 2
timeline.body: Total dose: 20 Gy, Dose per fraction: 4 Gy, Fractions: 5, Schedule: Over 1–2 weeks, Indication: Various lesions; equally effective as more fractionated regimens [KS-E, 1,3].

timeline.title: Regimen: Standard regimen
timeline.body: Total dose: 24 Gy, Dose per fraction: 2 Gy, Fractions: 12, Schedule: Over 2–3 weeks, Indication: Commonly used; effective for many lesions [KS-E, 1].

timeline.title: Regimen: Moderate fractionation
timeline.body: Total dose: 30 Gy, Dose per fraction: 2–3 Gy, Fractions: 10–15, Schedule: Over 2–4 weeks, Indication: More extensive or deeply invasive lesions [KS-E, 2,3].

timeline.title: Regimen: Higher dose regimen
timeline.body: Total dose: 40 Gy, Dose per fraction: 2 Gy, Fractions: 20, Schedule: Over 4 weeks, Indication: When more durable local response is desired [KS-E, 5].
